all.ALLSKY_SFC_SW_DWN: All Sky Surface Shortwave Downward Radiation (kWh/m²/day)

param_descriptions.T2M: Average Air Temperature at 2 m (°C): Modeled daily average air temperature. Data is obtained from the NASA MERRA-2 assimilation model (1981-Present, ~0.5°x0.625° resolution). In agriculture, T2M is critical for calculating Growing Degree Days (GDD) and modeling crop developmental stages and growth rates. Used in machine learning models for yield prediction and thermal stress assessment.
param_descriptions.T2M_MAX: Maximum Air Temperature at 2 m (°C): Modeled maximum hourly air temperature. Data is obtained from the NASA MERRA-2 assimilation model (1981-Present, ~0.5°x0.625° resolution). T2M_MAX is vital for detecting heat stress events which can cause flower abortion, reduced photosynthesis, and poor grain filling. Used as a feature in ML models to flag high-temperature risk periods.
param_descriptions.T2M_MIN: Minimum Air Temperature at 2 m (°C): Modeled minimum hourly air temperature. Data is obtained from the NASA MERRA-2 assimilation model (1981-Present, ~0.5°x0.625° resolution). T2M_MIN is essential for identifying frost risk (chilling injury) and for calculating GDD base temperatures. Used in phenology models to predict planting/harvest windows.
param_descriptions.T2M_RANGE: Diurnal Temperature Range at 2 m (°C): The difference between T2M_MAX and T2M_MIN. Data is derived from the NASA MERRA-2 model (1981-Present). A large range can impact evapotranspiration (ET) and plant respiration efficiency. It is used in data science for calculating stress indices and ET estimations.
param_descriptions.T2MWET: Wet Bulb Temperature at 2 m (°C): The adiabatic saturation temperature (cooling limit by evaporation). Data is obtained from the NASA MERRA-2 model (1981-Present). T2MWET is a measure of combined heat and humidity, highly relevant for assessing potential animal heat stress in livestock operations and for calculating cooling needs in controlled environments.
param_descriptions.T2MDEW: Dew Point Temperature at 2 m (°C): The temperature at which air becomes saturated and condensation forms. Data is obtained from the NASA MERRA-2 model (1981-Present). This parameter is crucial for predicting conditions conducive to foliar fungal diseases (e.g., rusts, blights) where prolonged leaf wetness is required for spore germination.
param_descriptions.TS: Surface Skin Temperature (°C): The calculated temperature of the top surface of the Earth (soil, vegetation canopy, or water). Data is obtained from the NASA MERRA-2 model (1981-Present). TS is a key input for remote sensing-based Evapotranspiration (ET) models (e.g., SEBAL/METRIC), and for monitoring soil temperature influence on seed germination and root growth.
param_descriptions.RH2M: Relative Humidity at 2 m (%): Ratio of actual water vapor content to the maximum possible saturation. Data is obtained from the NASA MERRA-2 model (1981-Present, ~0.5°x0.625° resolution). RH2M affects transpiration rates (low RH increases water loss) and disease incidence. Used in ML models for predicting irrigation needs and disease risk.
param_descriptions.PRECTOTCORR: Corrected Total Precipitation (mm/day): Total liquid and frozen water precipitation, adjusted for known biases. Data is obtained from the NASA MERRA-2 model (1981/1997-Present, ~0.5°x0.625° resolution). Essential for water balance models, irrigation scheduling, and assessing drought/flood risk. It's a primary input feature for deep learning models predicting crop yield.
param_descriptions.ALLSKY_SFC_SW_DWN: All Sky Surface Shortwave Downward Radiation (kWh/m²/day): Total solar energy reaching the surface under actual atmospheric conditions (Direct + Diffuse). Data is derived from NASA satellite observations (1984-Present, ~1.0°x1.0° resolution). This is the key energy input for calculating Reference Evapotranspiration (ETo) using methods like Penman-Monteith, which drives irrigation decisions.
param_descriptions.ALLSKY_SFC_PAR_TOT: All Sky Photosynthetically Active Radiation (MJ/m²/day): The total energy flux (400-700 nm) used by plants for photosynthesis. Data is derived from NASA satellite observations (1984-Present, ~1.0°x1.0° resolution). This directly quantifies the light available for plant growth. Used in Crop Growth Models (CGMs) like DSSAT and APSIM to simulate biomass accumulation and final yield.
param_descriptions.WS10M: Average Wind Speed at 10 m (m/s): Modeled daily average horizontal movement of air. Data is obtained from the NASA MERRA-2 model (1981-Present). Wind is a crucial factor in the Evapotranspiration (ET) calculation and can cause mechanical stress. Used in predictive models for water loss and general crop stress.
param_descriptions.WS10M_MAX: Maximum Wind Speed at 10 m (m/s): Modeled maximum instantaneous wind speed. Data is obtained from the NASA MERRA-2 model (1981-Present). Essential for assessing potential structural damage to crops (lodging of cereals/maize) or infrastructure (greenhouses). Used as a threshold trigger for high-risk warnings in decision-support systems.
param_descriptions.WD10M: Average Wind Direction at 10 m (degrees): Modeled daily average direction from which the wind is blowing. Data is obtained from the NASA MERRA-2 model (1981-Present). Primarily used for modeling the drift/dispersion of airborne inputs (pesticides, herbicides, spores) and for local climate pattern analysis (e.g., windbreak planning).
param_descriptions.PS: Surface Pressure (kPa): The atmospheric pressure at the earth's surface, adjusted for local elevation. Data is obtained from the NASA MERRA-2 model (1981-Present). PS is a necessary input for the Penman-Monteith Evapotranspiration equation to accurately account for air density, making it fundamental for precise water management.
